seo.page_title: Toyota Alphard 2018 import to NZ | Japan auctions
seo.meta_description: Import a 2018 Toyota Alphard from Japan with clear landed pricing, full auction documents and NZ-based support from search to compliance.
seo.h1_tag: Import a 2018 Toyota Alphard to NZ
seo.og_title: Import a 2018 Toyota Alphard to NZ
seo.og_description: Looking at a 2018 Toyota Alphard? We source from Japanese auctions, share the paperwork, and map out your likely landed cost before you bid.

blocks.hero_h1: Import a 2018 Toyota Alphard to NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: Bring in a 2018 Toyota Alphard from Japan with clear pricing, full auction paperwork and support from an NZ-based team that’s been doing this since 2009.
proof_points: Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: Many Kiwi families like the 2018 Toyota Alphard for its space, sliding doors and comfortable ride on longer trips. It works well for school runs, sports gear and road trips without feeling like a bus to drive.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes — from Japan auction to NZ road-ready
savings_block.body: For a 2018 Toyota Alphard, you start with the Japanese auction hammer price, then add shipping, marine insurance, NZ port charges, entry certification, compliance work and our service fee to reach a realistic landed cost. We lay out each line item before you bid so you can see how the total comes together, rather than working backwards from a single lump sum.

cta_form.headline: Find your Toyota Alphard 2018 — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Walk through a 2018 Toyota Alphard cabin and see how the sliding doors, three-row layout and rear seats work in real life before you decide to bid.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The 2018 Toyota Alphard is a large people mover that focuses on comfort and flexibility. With three rows of seating and wide-opening sliding doors, it’s easy for kids, grandparents or clients to get in and out. Japanese-market Alphards often come with features like dual-zone climate control and electric doors, which make day-to-day use simpler. Imported carefully, it’s a practical way to get a high-spec family van without starting at local yard prices.

buyer_persona.headline: Who is this car right for?
buyer_persona.body: You’re moving people more than gear — family, friends, or clients — and want something that’s easy to live with every day. You don’t mind a larger vehicle if it’s comfortable, quiet and well equipped. If you’re happy to trade a bit of fuel use for space and comfort, a 2018 Alphard is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We’ve been helping Kiwis bring in cars from Japan since 2009, handling hundreds of vehicles across family, commercial and enthusiast segments. Our role is to translate the Japanese auction system into clear options, pricing and risks so you can make an informed call. You see the same auction sheets we do, along with our notes on condition, mileage and any comments. A real NZ team stays in touch from first quote through to compliance and handover.

car_specs_benefits.spec: 2018 model year Toyota Alphard (Japanese domestic market)
car_specs_benefits.benefit: Gives you the later-shape body and updated safety and comfort features compared with earlier Alphards.
Carries a full family or work group in one vehicle, instead of running two smaller cars.
Makes school runs, tight carparks and kerbside drop-offs easier and safer for kids and older passengers.
Simple to service in NZ and easy for any driver to hop in and go, whether it’s around town or on the open road.
Often includes extras like climate control and powered doors that make daily use more relaxed on longer NZ trips.

cta_mid_page.headline: Start a 2018 Alphard search today
cta_mid_page.subtext: Tell us what spec and budget you have in mind and we’ll come back with real auction options and likely landed costs.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how 2018 Toyota Alphard options from Japan typically differ when we’re shortlisting cars.

rows.notes: You’re looking at 2018-build Alphards, which generally meet current NZ entry standards if ESC and safety features are present and documentation is clean.

Japanese 2018 Alphards are usually offered with different petrol engine sizes and front- or all-wheel drive. We’ll outline the pros and cons of each option for your driving mix before you decide what to target.
Higher trims can add features like upgraded seats, extra airbags, more speakers and additional convenience tech. We flag these on each auction sheet so you can see what you’re paying for.

We check for ESC, airbag and safety system notes on the auction sheet and export certificate. Based on auction documentation, this model usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no structural issues are noted.
2018 Alphards appear regularly across the main Japanese auctions, but specific colours, trims or low-mileage examples can take longer to find and may cost more.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For a 2018 Alphard, we focus on mileage, interior wear and any notes about previous use so you’re not surprised later.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common trims or colours, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a 2018 Alphard from Japan is straightforward, but it helps to understand the moving parts.

items.risk: Worrying about buying a people mover you haven’t seen in person.
items.solution: We base our advice on the Japanese auction sheet, photos and sale history, and talk you through any comments on condition or repairs before you decide to bid.

items.risk: Concern about hidden costs on top of the auction price.
items.solution: We map out a line-by-line estimate covering auction price, shipping, insurance, port charges, entry certification, compliance work and our fee so you can see where the money goes.

items.risk: Not being sure what the auction grade really means for condition.
items.solution: We explain the grading system in plain language, highlight any notes about panels, paint or interior wear, and share the original documents so you can judge the trade-offs.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the sort of 2018 Alphard you want, your budget and how you’ll use it. We watch the Japanese auctions, filter out poor candidates and send you the best options with clear landed cost estimates. You choose whether to bid, and we handle the logistics and paperwork from Japan to NZ compliance.

faq_block.q: Is a 2018 Toyota Alphard a good option for a NZ family?
faq_block.a: For many families, the 2018 Alphard offers a lot of space, sliding doors and a comfortable ride for longer trips. The trade-off is that it’s a larger vehicle to park and will use more fuel than a small hatchback.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2018 Alphard from Japan?
faq_block.a: Total cost depends on the auction hammer price, mileage, trim level and shipping at the time. On top of the auction price you’ll have shipping, insurance, NZ port charges, entry certification, compliance work, registration and our service fee.

faq_block.q: Will a 2018 Alphard meet NZ entry and WoF standards?
faq_block.a: Most 2018 Alphards are built to a high safety standard, but NZ entry certification still depends on the individual car. Based on auction documentation, this model usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications or structural issues are noted.

faq_block.q: How long does it take from winning an auction to driving in NZ?
faq_block.a: From a successful bid, shipping to NZ typically takes 2–4 weeks depending on the vessel schedule. Allow additional time in NZ for port processing, entry certification, compliance work and registration before you can drive it home.

faq_block.q: What condition can I expect from a Japanese auction Alphard?
faq_block.a: Condition varies by grade, mileage and previous use. The auction sheet lists exterior and interior grades, panel notes and comments from the inspector, and we translate that into plain English with our own view before you commit.

footer_cta.headline: Ready to look at 2018 Alphard options?
footer_cta.subtext: Tell us what you’re after and we’ll come back with real Japanese auction listings, clear landed cost estimates and next steps if you want to move ahead.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get updates on fresh 2018 Alphard listings, price trends and import tips direct from the Japanese auctions.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based team specialising in sourcing vehicles from Japanese auctions and dealers. Since 2009 we’ve helped Kiwis bring in family cars, commercial vehicles and enthusiast models with clear pricing and full documentation. We focus on explaining the process and risks so you can decide what’s right for you.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost for a 2018 Alphard covers more than just the auction hammer price.

rows.notes: The winning bid in Japan, plus local auction fees and inland transport if needed.
Ocean freight from Japan to NZ and marine insurance to cover the vehicle while it’s in transit.
Port charges, MPI checks, entry certification, any compliance repairs, registration and our service fee to manage the process.

auction_checklist.why: Gives a quick view of overall condition and how hard the Alphard has been worked in Japan.

auction_checklist.check: Panel and rust notes on the auction sheet
auction_checklist.why: Flags repairs, dents or corrosion that could affect how the car looks and how it’s treated at NZ entry certification.
A people mover’s cabin gets a lot of use, so seat wear and trim marks tell you how it’s been treated by previous owners.

auction_checklist.check: Service stickers and records in photos
auction_checklist.why: Oil change stickers and stamped books can indicate regular maintenance, which matters for long-term reliability.

auction_checklist.check: Tyres and underbody photos where available
auction_checklist.why: Can hint at alignment issues, suspension wear or rust that might not be obvious from exterior shots alone.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You want a very small, easy-to-park city car with low fuel use above all else.
You’re not comfortable driving or parking a larger vehicle in tight streets or older garages.
You need a vehicle with heavy towing capacity or commercial load space rather than people-carrying ability.
You’re expecting a near-new van on a very tight budget and don’t want to compromise on mileage or cosmetic condition.

alternatives_block.intro: If a 2018 Alphard isn’t quite right, there are other Japanese people movers and vans that can work better for some situations.

rows.better_if: You like the Alphard layout but prefer a different front-end styling and similar comfort features.
rows.tradeoff: Very similar mechanically, so pricing and running costs are broadly alike.

engine_drivetrain_block.headline: Engine and drivetrain considerations
engine_drivetrain_block.body: The 2018 Toyota Alphard range in Japan is built around smooth petrol engines paired with automatic transmissions, often with the option of front- or all-wheel drive. When we review auction listings, we confirm engine size, drivetrain layout and any comments about leaks, noises or warning lights noted by the inspector. That way you can weigh up performance, fuel use and traction against how and where you’ll be driving in NZ.
checklist: Confirm engine size and code from the auction sheet and export certificate.
Check for any notes about oil leaks, engine noise or warning lights.
Look at drivetrain type (2WD vs 4WD) and match it to your typical driving.
Review transmission comments for shift quality or known issues.
Consider future servicing — ask your local mechanic which variants they see most often.

fuel_use_block.headline: Fuel use and running costs
fuel_use_block.body: A 2018 Alphard is a sizeable petrol people mover, so it will use more fuel than a compact hatch or small SUV. In return you get space, comfort and flexibility for family or work trips. We can help you compare likely fuel use between engine options and talk through whether your driving is mostly short city hops or longer open-road runs, which can make a difference to real-world consumption.
items: Larger body and petrol engine mean higher fuel use than smaller cars.
Engine size and drivetrain choice can change how often you’re at the pump.
Regular servicing and correct tyre pressures help keep consumption in check.
If fuel cost is your top concern, we can discuss smaller or hybrid alternatives.

service_history_block.headline: Service history and maintenance
service_history_block.body: Japanese auction cars often show service stickers, books or records in the photos, but the detail can vary from car to car. We look for signs of regular oil changes and workshop visits, then recommend a full service and fluid change once the Alphard arrives in NZ. From there, sticking to a sensible service schedule with a workshop familiar with Japanese imports will help keep it running well.
items: Check auction photos for service stickers on the door jamb or under the bonnet.
Look for stamped service books or invoices where available.
Plan for a full service, fluid change and fresh filters on arrival in NZ.
Use quality oil and parts suited to Japanese petrol engines.
Keep records of NZ servicing to support future resale.

mechanical_risks_block.headline: Mechanical risks to be aware of
mechanical_risks_block.body: Any used import carries some mechanical risk, even with good paperwork. With a 2018 Alphard, the main things to watch are wear items like suspension, brakes and tyres, plus any signs of hard family or commercial use. We use the auction sheet and photos to flag obvious issues, but some items only show up once the car is on a hoist in NZ.
items: Higher-mileage examples may have more suspension and brake wear, adding to early maintenance costs.
People movers can show interior wear, broken trim or tired seat mechanisms from heavy family use.
Undisclosed minor repairs or paintwork may be noted only briefly on the auction sheet.
Mechanical issues that develop in transit or after arrival are handled like any used car — through inspection and repair, not factory warranty.
Budgeting a buffer for initial maintenance and tyres is sensible when importing any used vehicle.

model_deep_dive_block.headline: 2018 Toyota Alphard: what you’re really getting
model_deep_dive_block.body: The 2018 Alphard sits in Toyota’s line-up as a premium people mover aimed at comfort and refinement. In Japan it’s often used as a family hauler or for executive transport, so many examples come with well-kept interiors and extra convenience features. When we shortlist cars, we look at how it’s been used, how clean the cabin is, and whether the options match what you actually need in NZ rather than just chasing the highest trim name.
items: Spacious three-row cabin that can be configured for passengers or folded for extra cargo.
Sliding doors and a relatively low step-in height make access easy for kids and older passengers.
Japanese-market features can include climate control, power doors and upgraded seating.
Later-shape 2018 models benefit from updated safety and comfort tech compared with older Alphards.
Ideal for families, airport transfers or business use where comfort matters more than outright load space.

trim_comparison_block.headline: Understanding 2018 Alphard trims from Japan
trim_comparison_block.body: Toyota uses a mix of trim names and option packs on the 2018 Alphard, and they don’t always line up neatly with NZ models. Rather than focusing only on the badge, we break down what each car actually has in terms of seats, safety gear and comfort features so you can decide what’s worth paying for.
items: Some trims focus on comfort with upgraded seats and interior materials.
Others add extra safety features or driver aids that can be useful on NZ roads.
Wheel size, lighting and exterior styling details can change between trims.
We highlight key differences on each auction listing so you can compare value, not just names.
